data.title: 10 Industrials Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
data.description:
This whale alert can help traders discover the next big trading opportunities.

Whales are entities with large sums of money and we track their transactions here at Benzinga on our options activity scanner.

Traders often look for circumstances when the market estimation of an option diverges away from its normal worth. Abnormal amounts of trading activity could push option prices to hyperbolic or underperforming levels.

Below are some instances of options activity happening in the Industrials sector:

Explanation

These itemized elaborations have been created using the accompanying table.

• Regarding UBER (NYSE:UBER), we observe a call option trade with bullish sentiment. It expires in 688 day(s) on December 17, 2027. Parties traded 100 contract(s) at a $140.00 strike. The total cost received by the writing party (or parties) was $49.0K, with a price of $490.0 per contract. There were 1736 open contracts at this strike prior to today, and today 700 contract(s) were bought and sold.

Options Alert Terminology
- Call Contracts: The right to buy shares as indicated in the contract.
- Put Contracts: The right to sell shares as indicated in the contract.
- Expiration Date: When the contract expires. One must act on the contract by this date if one wants to use it.
- Premium/Option Price: The price of the contract.

For more information, visit our Guide to Understanding Options Alerts or read more news on unusual options activity.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.

data.title: There's Now a Record Number of 401(k) Millionaires — But Can You Guess the Average Balance? Hint: It's Nowhere Near $1 Million
data.description:
Millionaires are multiplying inside America's 401(k)s, and headlines are throwing a party. But buried beneath the celebration is a less glamorous stat: the average balance is still barely over six figures.

Fidelity's latest report, released in September, shows a record-breaking 654,000 accounts now hold over $1 million, a nearly 10% jump from the previous quarter. It's the highest number ever recorded. 

Yet despite the surge, the average 401(k) balance sits at just $144,400—a record in its own right, but still a fraction of what the wealthiest accounts hold.

Don't Miss:

  • Missed Nvidia and Tesla? RAD Intel Could Be the Next AI Powerhouse — Just $0.85 a Share
  • Warren Buffett once said, "If you don't find a way to make money while you sleep, you will work until you die." Here’s how you can earn passive income with just $10, starting today.

Americans are "continuing to exhibit impactful savings behaviors such as staying the course and focusing on long-term goals, which clearly is having a positive effect on retirement savings," according to Fidelity Investments President of Workplace Investing Sharon Brovelli. "To see balances and saving behaviors increase across all savings vehicles is encouraging, especially as savers continue to navigate an uncertain economic environment."

The Rise of the 401(k) Elite

Fidelity reports that 401(k) millionaires surged from 595,000 to 654,000 in a single quarter, driven by steady contributions and market momentum. But that success story mostly belongs to a small group of high earners and long-term savers.

For everyone else, growth is more gradual.

Current averages for other retirement plans:

  • IRA accounts average $137,902
  • 403(b) plans, commonly used by nonprofit and education workers, average $131,200

Trending: Americans With a Financial Plan Can 4X Their Wealth — Get Your Personalized Plan from a CFP Pro

No median values were included, but they would almost certainly come in lower—especially for younger participants or those just getting started.

Glimpses of Progress

Still, long-term savers are seeing meaningful progress. Women who've contributed to their 401(k) for 15 consecutive years now average $501,100, the first time Fidelity has recorded this group crossing the half-million mark. That's a 16.5% increase over the previous year.

Meanwhile, younger investors are reshaping retirement strategies. According to Fidelity, 95% of Gen Z's 401(k) contributions now go to Roth accounts, a higher share than both Millennials at 75% and Gen X at 66%.

The average total savings rate, including employer matches, remained steady at 14.2%—enough, Fidelity says, to keep most consistent savers on track.

What the Numbers Reveal

The record number of 401(k) millionaires is worth noting—but so is the reality that most accounts are still far from that milestone. Averages don't tell the whole story, especially when a few high balances can dramatically skew the data.

Whether someone is already in the millionaire camp or closer to the national average, consulting a financial advisor can help create a strategy that bridges the gap. With personalized guidance on contribution levels, asset allocation, and long-term goals, even modest accounts can gain momentum over time.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: FDA Clinical Hold Knocks REGENXBIO Stock
data.description:
REGENXBIO Inc. (NASDAQ:RGNX) shares are tumbling on Wednesday with a session volume of 3.83 million versus an average volume of 684.271 thousand.

The move follows a regulatory update that has raised concerns among investors. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) placed a clinical hold on its investigational gene therapies.

FDA Holds REGENXBIO’s Gene Therapy Programs

The FDA’s decision affects REGENXBIO’s RGX-111 and RGX-121 programs, which are aimed at treating rare genetic disorders.

The clinical hold was prompted by a case of neoplasm detected in a participant treated with RGX-111, leading to scrutiny over the safety of both therapies.

RGX-111 is under development for MPS I, also known as Hurler syndrome, which is an inherited condition caused by a faulty gene. Children with Hurler syndrome lack an enzyme that the body needs to digest sugar.

A neoplasm is a new and abnormal growth of tissue in a part of the body, especially as a characteristic of cancer.

The FDA also placed a clinical hold on RGX-121 for MPS II, also known as Hunter Syndrome, citing the similarities in products, study populations, and shared risk between the clinical studies.

Children with Hunter syndrome lack an enzyme called iduronate 2-sulfatase that the body needs to digest sugar. As a result, undigested sugar molecules build up in the body.

In addition, the company reported that no evidence of neoplasm has been found in other participants treated with RGX-111 or RGX-121.

REGENXBIO’s CEO expressed surprise at the FDA’s decision, emphasizing the favorable safety profile of RGX-121 based on data from over 30 patients treated, some nearly seven years ago.

RGNX Stock Faces Short-Term Weakness

The stock is currently trading 23.4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 9.9% below its 100-day SMA, demonstrating significant short-term weakness. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 44.41% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 45.56, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $12.00
  • Key Support: $11.00

Upcoming Earnings: Analysts’ Eyes on RGNX

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on March 12, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 86 cents (Up from $-1.01 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $50.09 million (Up from $21.21 million YoY)

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $31.38. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Stifel: Buy (Raises Target to $45.00).
  • Leerink Partners: Outperform (Raises Target to $20.00).
  • Chardan Capital: Buy (Maintains Target to $52.00).

RGNX Scores High on Benzinga Edge Momentum

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for REGENXBIO, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 91.62) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: REGENXBIO’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score of 91.62, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the broader market despite the recent regulatory challenges. Investors should remain cautious as the company addresses the FDA’s concerns while monitoring the stock’s performance closely.

RGNX Price Action: Regenxbio shares were down 20.21% at $10.70 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Intel Stock Surges Wednesday: Here's What Investors Are Watching
data.description:
Intel Corp (NASDAQ:INTC) shares are trading higher Wednesday afternoon amid insider buying and strength in chip stocks.

Semiconductor stocks are moving higher Wednesday, fueled by strong quarterly results from ASML and an upbeat near-term outlook from Texas Instruments, reinforcing broader optimism across the technology sector. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Intel stock is showing exceptional strength. What’s driving INTC stock higher?

Insider Buying Hints At $42 Support

Sentiment around Intel soured last week after management warned of a first-quarter revenue slowdown and potential chip supply shortages.

Earnings Beat, But Guidance And Capacity Weigh

Intel's fourth-quarter report modestly topped expectations, with adjusted earnings of 15 cents per share on revenue near $13.7 billion. However, management guided first-quarter revenue to roughly $11.7 billion to $12.7 billion and breakeven adjusted earnings, both below Wall Street forecasts.

Analysts flagged internal wafer-capacity constraints and a less favorable product mix as key headwinds, even as data-center and AI demand stayed resilient.

Intel Stock Technical Analysis: Strong Trend With Mixed Momentum Signals

Intel is currently trading 10.7% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 31.2% above its 100-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 146.97% and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting a robust upward trend.

The RSI is at 50.19, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum for Intel, indicating that traders should watch for potential shifts in either direction.

  • Key Resistance: $54.50
  • Key Support: $44.00

INTC’s Business Model

Intel is a leading digital chipmaker focused on designing and manufacturing microprocessors for the global personal computer and data center markets. Intel pioneered the x86 architecture for microprocessors and led the semiconductor industry down the path of Moore’s law for advances in semiconductor manufacturing.

The company’s significance is underscored by its market share leadership in central processing units in both the PC and server end markets.

INTC Shares Surge Wednesday

INTC Price Action: Intel shares were up 11.06% at $48.79 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
